descript.abstract: World Administrative Units, 2022 represents the boundaries for the first-level administrative divisions of the world.
descript.purpose: World Administrative Divisions provides a detailed basemap layer for the country first-level administrative divisions of the world. It has been designed to be used as a basemap and includes a Disputed Boundaries layer that can be used to edit boundaries to fit your needs. Included are fields for name and ISO codes, along with notes identifying disputed boundaries and continent information. Particularly useful are the LAND_TYPE and LAND_RANK fields that separate polygons based on their size. These fields are helpful for rendering at different scales by providing the ability to turn off small islands that may clutter small-scale views.

cntinfo.cntinst:
In the United States– Direct all inquiries regarding software/data pricing and consulting services to your local Esri Regional Office. For support, contact Esri by telephone (voice) between 5:00 a.m. and 5:00 p.m. Pacific time, Monday through Friday, by dialing +1-888-377-4575; facsimile (fax) available at +1-909-792-0960; or visit https://support.esri.com/; Esri holidays excluded. Email and chat are available through the Support page on My Esri (https://my.esri.com).
Outside the United States– Direct all inquiries regarding software/data pricing, sales, support, and consulting services to your local Esri Office. This information can be found at https://www.esri.com/en-us/contact#c=us&t=0. For other questions or comments, you may contact Esri headquarters by email, telephone, or fax or write to us.

dataqual.logic: The dataset is converted to file geodatabase (FGDB) format using tools in ArcGIS. The geometry is checked, and if needed repaired.
dataqual.complete: After processing, the dataset is checked for drawing display and number of records and file sizes compared with source materials.

horizpa.horizpar: The World Base Map was derived using Landsat 8, Landsat 7 (Global Land Survey (GLS) 2000 and GLS 2005), and SRTM (Shuttle Radar Topography Mission) as a global base. Landsat 7 provided the horizontal positional accuracy of ± 50 meters 90% circular error, and SRTM ensured that features appropriately fit the topography (such as rivers flowed through valleys).

serinfo.sername: A subset of World Base Map.

procstep.procdesc: The following steps were performed by Esri: Extracted the file geodatabase (.gdb) feature class from the subset of World Base Map. Removed all first-level administrative subdivisions. Updated country names as needed. Ran the Erase tool using polygons from the World Base Map Hydropolys feature class to remove the areas that represent seas and large lakes that cross international borders. Added the fields COUNTRY and CONTINENT and coded them from source fields in the World Base Map Country_Boundaries feature class. Added the field ISO_CODE and coded it by combining the values of the fields ISO_CC and ISO_SUB. Added the field ADMINTYPE and coded it by removing the administrative types from the field NOTES. Added the fields LAND_TYPE and LAND_RANK and manually determined their values from the Shape_Area field. Deleted fields not needed. Dissolved on all fields to make multipart features for the fields LAND_TYPE and LAND_RANK.

altsys.altenc: Explicit elevation coordinate included with horizontal coordinates

enttyp.enttypd: The polygons represent the boundaries for the first-level administrative divisions of the world.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: The country in which the administrative division is located.
The combined International Organization for Standardization (ISO) country and country subdivision codes for the administrative division.
The ISO country code for the country in which the administrative division is located.
The ISO country subdivision code for the administrative division.
The administrative type for the administrative division.
The indicator that the administrative division is disputed.

edom.edomvd: The administrative division is not disputed.

attr.attrdef: The notes that provide details about the administrative division regarding disputes, claims, and descriptions.
The indicator that the administrative division is autonomous.

edom.edomvd: The administrative division is not autonomous.

attr.attrdef: The sovereign country affiliated with the administrative division.
The continent on which the administrative division is located.

edom.edomv: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Australia, Europe, North America, Oceania, South America

attr.attrdef: The land type of the administrative division based on its areal size. This field is useful for rendering at different scales.

edom.edomv: Primary land (The continental mainland or selected island that creates a world view at very small scales), Large island, Medium island, Small island, and Very small island

attr.attrdef: The land rank of the administrative division based on its areal size. This field is useful for rendering at different scales.

edom.edomvd: Primary land - the continental mainland or selected island that creates a world view at very small scales.

attr.attrdef: Length of feature in internal units.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Area of feature in internal units squared.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

cntinfo.cntinst:
In the United States, contact the Esri Sales staff at +1-800-447-9778 or visit https://www.esri.com/en-us/contact#c=us&t=1 for more information about our software and data.
Outside the United States, direct all inquiries to your local Esri Office. This information can be found at https://www.esri.com/en-us/contact#c=us&t=0.

distinfo.distliab: See access and use constraints information.

digtinfo.formcont: To use this data requires software that works with FGDB files.

stdorder.ordering: Data and Maps is available only as part of ArcGIS software.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
